data.title: Iran claims control after bloody crackdown. How will the U.S. respond?
data.description:
Security forces have "full control" of the Islamic Republic, according to Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi, following two weeks of protests and unrest. Reports suggest otherwise, but it is hard to discern information with an internet blackout and other communications largely cut off from the outside world. Various death toll figures of civilians have also been cited, ranging from several hundred to at least 12,000.

What's next: President Trump is still weighing a response that can include military strikes or cyberattacks, but in the meantime, he has opened the channel for diplomacy while hitting the country economically. "Effective immediately, any Country doing business with the Islamic Republic of Iran will pay a Tariff of 25% on any and all business being done with the United States of America," he wrote on Truth Social. "This Order is final and conclusive. Thank you for your attention to this matter!" See Iran's top trading partners

Oil is another big risk if Supreme Leader Khamenei decides to disrupt traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, and may also explain recent U.S. moves in Venezuela that can help shore up supply. About a quarter of the world’s seaborne oil passes through the critical chokepoint, as well as significant LNG exports. Any action brings its own risks, given all of the parties involved, and whether that will ultimately be enough to address Iran's currency crisis and economic mismanagement that triggered the protests in the first place.

Thought bubble: While some kind of military action is likely, President Trump doesn't necessarily want regime change, but rather a regime that will be submissive to America's global priorities and leadership. In fact, it's a playbook that's already in place even for allies, with Trump preferring administrations where he can make demands from a "stronger" place of power, rather than coming to the table from a "weaker" place of shared values. It also helps understand why he wants current governments, like the ones in Venezuela, Cuba, and Syria, to remain intact. That can all change in Iran if the population successfully fights for new leadership, but unless there is broad and visible support, don't expect Trump to go all in on "failed" regime change.

data.title: Black Rifle Coffee expects to meet FY2025 sales, adj. EBITDA guidance
data.description:
• Black Rifle Coffee (BRCC) expects to meet its previously issued guidance for fiscal year 2025, with revenue of at least $395M ($395.40M estimated) and adjusted EBITDA of at least $20M.
  • The company also expects to record a non-cash impairment of around $1.4M related to raw material inputs associated with a formulation change.
  • This impairment reflects excess inventory rendered unusable by the formulation change and does not reflect underlying consumer demand or operating performance.
  • As a result of this non-cash charge, the company now expects fiscal year 2025 gross margin to be ~34.5% to 34.7% vs. the previously expected gross margin of at least 35%.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: L3Harris stock rises on proposed $1B Department of Defense investment
data.description:
L3Harris Technologies (LHX) shares popped 4.2% premarket on Tuesday after the defense contractor announced a proposed $1B investment from the Department of War ((DoW)) to significantly increase capacity to build solid rocket motors that power vital U.S. and allied missiles.

L3Harris and the DoD have agreed to a proposed $1 billion investment in L3Harris’ Missile Solutions business through a convertible preferred security that would convert into common shares upon an IPO.

Under the deal, L3Harris plans to take the Missile Solutions unit public in the second half of 2026, creating a standalone company focused on critical propulsion systems.

“We’re taking action to build today’s ‘Arsenal of Freedom’ by launching a pure-play missile solutions provider,” said Christopher Kubasik, chairman and CEO, L3Harris. “Recent Trump Administration actions have placed renewed emphasis on strengthening the defense industrial base and reinvigorating competition following a 30-year wave of consolidation. Building on several years of sustained investment and operational improvements by L3Harris, this new company will serve as a key partner to the DoW in supporting efforts to deter and defeat America’s adversaries.”

Following these transactions, L3Harris (LHX) will retain a controlling interest in the Missile Solutions business. The company will host an investor conference to discuss the partnership at 9 AM ET today.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Bessent, allies discuss diversifying rare earth supply chains away from China
data.description:
Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Monday convened a meeting of finance ministers from several countries to discuss ways to diversify rare earth supply chains and reduce reliance on China.

The meeting was held in Washington and included ministers from Australia, Canada, Mexico, France, Germany, the UK, Japan, South Korea and India. The group together represents about 60% of the global demand for critical minerals.

"I am optimistic that nations will pursue prudent derisking over decoupling and understand well the need for decisive action," Bessent posted on X after the meeting.

The officials discussed reducing dependence on China's rare earths through moves like setting a price floor, securing alternative supplies, tax and financial incentives, and tariff measures. 

The meeting follows Beijing's move to ban exports of dual-use items to Japan, including certain rare earth magnets.

While G7 leaders agreed last year to strengthen supply chain security, Washington is reportedly frustrated by their slow pace of action and continued reliance on China.

data.title: Central bankers across the globe plan to back Fed's Powell - report
data.description:
Global central bank officials are planning to release a statement to support Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell amid threats from the Trump administration, including a criminal probe over Fed headquarters renovations, Reuters reported, citing two anonymous sources.

The planned statement will be signed by central bankers from around the world; however, it has undergone extensive changes over the ‍past day, and it remains unclear how many would initially sign, the sources said.

The Trump administration launched a criminal investigation into Powell, which he described as an attempt to pressure the Fed on monetary policy. This has sparked widespread criticism from financial leaders, raising fears of eroding central bank independence.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Delta Air Lines ahead of Q4: SA Quant bullish, Wall Street even more so
data.description:
Ahead of Q4 earnings, Delta Air Lines (DAL) is broadly favored across rating frameworks, with Seeking Alpha Quant (Buy), Seeking Alpha analysts (Buy), and Wall Street analysts (Strong Buy) all aligned.

The airline is expected to report EPS of $1.55 on revenue of $15.69B.

Valuation stands out as a key positive, SA Quant model explains, with DAL trading at a P/E of ~10, about 61% cheaper than the sector median of 26, suggesting the stock remains attractively priced. Growth is mixed, as free cash flow per share rose 24%, well above the sector’s 9%, though year-over-year EPS growth remains negative. Profitability is solid, with a 7% net income margin, topping the sector median of 6%.

Wall Street remains firmly bullish on Delta Air Lines (DAL), maintaining a Strong Buy rating. The stock is up 22.2% over the past six months, and analysts’ average price target of $79.86 implies a further 12.4% upside. Earnings revisions also lean positive, with 11 upward EPS revisions in the past three months compared with nine downward, pointing to improving sentiment ahead of results.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish, with a Buy rating on Delta Air Lines (DAL). “Delta Air Lines is rated a continued buy, with industry trends and company strategy driving strong revenue and margin prospects into 2026,” SA columnist Tim Dunn noted.

However, Vinay Utham cautions, "Delta Air Lines enters Q4 earnings with strong momentum but is rated HOLD due to marginal overvaluation and high expectations."

"For the company’s Q4 print, all eyes should be on whether the airline was able to capitalize on the strong holiday season and drive further growth from premiumization. The extent of damage from the government shutdown is the other factor that investors should watch out for in the Q4 earnings report."

data.title: Google to develop and make high-end phones in Vietnam this year - report
data.description:
• Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) will start developing and manufacturing high-end smartphones in Vietnam this year from scratch, Nikkei Asia reported, as U.S. companies take another step toward building supply chains fully outside of China.
  • Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) already mass-produces high-end smartphones in Vietnam and conducts some verification there, so building new phones from scratch in the country should be achievable, the report said.
  • Google will begin new product introductions (NPI) for its Pixel, Pixel Pro, and Pixel Fold phones in Vietnam, with development of the lower-end Pixel A series remaining in China for now.
